oneOf.description: Global variable defined in non-module scope

ignoreGlobs.description: Ignore globs within this library. eg: ["**/*.lua"]

anyOf.description: Library configuration with path and ignore rules

insertSpace.description: Add space after `---` comments when inserting `@diagnostic disable-next-line`.

autoRequire.description:
Automatically insert call to `require` when autocompletion
inserts objects from other modules.

autoRequireFunction.description: The function used for auto-requiring modules.

autoRequireNamingConvention.description: The naming convention for auto-required filenames.

autoRequireSeparator.description: A separator used in auto-require paths.

baseFunctionIncludesName.description: Whether to include the name in the base function completion. Effect: `function () end` -> `function name() end`.

callSnippet.description: Whether to use call snippets in completions.

postfix.description: Symbol that's used to trigger postfix autocompletion.

EmmyrcDiagnostic.description: Represents the diagnostic configuration for Emmyrc.

diagnosticInterval.description: Delay between opening/changing a file and scanning it for errors, in milliseconds.

disable.description: A list of diagnostic codes that are disabled.

enable.description: A flag indicating whether diagnostics are enabled.

enables.description: A list of diagnostic codes that are enabled.

globalsRegex.description: A list of regular expressions for global variables.

severity.description: A map of diagnostic codes to their severity settings.

privateName.description: Treat specific field names as private, e.g. `m_*` means `XXX.m_id` and `XXX.m_type` are private, witch can only be accessed in the class where the definition is located.

rstDefaultRole.description:
When `syntax` is `Myst` or `Rst`, specifies default role used
with RST processor.

rstPrimaryDomain.description:
When `syntax` is `Myst` or `Rst`, specifies primary domain used
with RST processor.

enable.description: Enable parsing strings for color tags and showing a color picker next to them.

args.description: The arguments to pass to the external tool.

program.description: The command to run the external tool.

timeout.description: The timeout for the external tool in milliseconds.

oneOf.description: When returning class definition, use class name, otherwise keep original name.
Prefer discovered .editorconfig files over .gluarc formatter overrides.
Prefer .gluarc formatter overrides over discovered .editorconfig files.
Use EmmyLuaCodeStyle built-in defaults. No style overrides are applied.
Apply the built-in CFC (Clockwork Fighters Community) style preset: 4-space indentation, spaces inside parentheses, semicolons replaced with newlines.
Apply no preset. Only the `styleOverrides` you configure explicitly take effect; everything else uses the formatter's compiled-in default.

EmmyrcFormatStyleOverrides.description: Typed EmmyLuaCodeStyle overrides for built-in formatting.

align_array_table.description: Array table layout: `none` keeps items inline, `always` puts each item on its own line, `contain_curly` uses multiline only when a nested table is present.

align_call_args.description: Vertically align arguments of multi-line function calls to the opening parenthesis.

align_chain_expr.description: Align chained method / field expressions: `none` disables, `always` aligns all chains, `only_call_stmt` aligns only call statement chains.

align_continuous_assign_statement.description: Align consecutive assignment statements on their `=` sign (`true` aligns within a block, `always` aligns across blank lines, `false` disables).

align_continuous_inline_comment.description: Align the `--` of inline comments across consecutive lines so they form a vertical column.

align_continuous_line_space.description: Maximum number of blank lines between consecutive statements before they are no longer considered part of the same aligned block.

align_continuous_rect_table_field.description: Align key-value fields in rectangular table literals so that `=` signs line up vertically.

align_continuous_similar_call_args.description: Align arguments across similar consecutive function calls so that matching argument positions line up vertically.

align_function_params.description: Vertically align parameters in multi-line function declarations to the opening parenthesis.

allow_non_indented_comments.description: Allow comments that start at column 0 to remain un-indented even when inside an indented block.

auto_collapse_lines.description: Collapse short multi-statement blocks into a single line when they fit within `max_line_length`.

break_all_list_when_line_exceed.description: When a line exceeds `max_line_length`, break every item in the containing list onto its own line rather than only the overflowing item.

break_before_braces.description: Place the opening `{` of a table constructor on a new line instead of at the end of the preceding line.

call_arg_parentheses.description: How to handle parentheses around single-argument function calls with a string or table literal (`keep`, `remove`, `remove_table_only`, `remove_string_only`).

continuation_indent.description: Number of extra spaces used for indenting continuation lines when a line is wrapped.

detect_end_of_line.description: Detect line endings from file content.

end_statement_with_semicolon.description: Semicolon handling: `keep` preserves existing, `always` adds to all statements, `same_line` adds between same-line statements, `replace_with_newline` replaces semicolons with newlines, `never` removes all.

ignore_space_after_colon.description: Do not normalize spacing after `:` in method call and definition syntax.

ignore_spaces_inside_function_call.description: When `true`, preserve existing spacing inside function call arguments instead of normalizing it.

indent_size.description: Number of spaces used for one indentation level.

insert_final_newline.description: Ensure file ends with a newline.

keep_indents_on_empty_lines.description: Keep indentation whitespace on otherwise blank lines instead of stripping it.

max_line_length.description: Column limit before the formatter wraps items onto new lines. Set to `"unset"` to disable line length enforcement.

never_indent_before_if_condition.description: Do not add extra indentation to wrapped `if`/`elseif` condition expressions.

never_indent_comment_on_if_branch.description: Do not indent a lone comment that appears inside an `if` branch body.

quote_style.description: Preferred quote style for string literals. `none` preserves the original style.

remove_call_expression_list_finish_comma.description: Remove trailing commas that appear at the end of function call argument lists.

space_after_comma.description: Add a space after commas in expressions and argument lists.

space_after_comma_in_for_statement.description: Add a space after commas in `for` loop range expressions.

space_after_comment_dash.description: Ensure a single space exists after the comment dash prefix (`--`).

space_around_assign_operator.description: Spaces around assignment operators (`=`). Accepts `true`/`false`, or `"none"` (no spaces), `"always"` (spaces on both sides), `"no_space_asym"` (asymmetric spacing).

space_around_concat_operator.description: Spaces around the concatenation operator (`..`). Accepts `true`/`false`, or `"none"` (no spaces), `"always"` (spaces on both sides), `"no_space_asym"` (space on left side only).

space_around_table_append_operator.description: Add spaces around the table append operator (`#t + 1` style appends).

space_around_table_field_list.description: Add spaces inside table braces, e.g. `{ a = 1 }` vs `{a = 1}`.

space_before_attribute.description: Add a space before Lua attribute annotations, e.g. `local x <const>`.

space_before_closure_open_parenthesis.description: Add a space before `(` in anonymous function expressions, e.g. `function ()`.

space_before_function_call_open_parenthe.description: Add a space before `(` in function calls, e.g. `print ('hello')`.

space_before_function_call_single_arg.description: Space handling before a single string or table literal argument: `always`, `only_string`, `only_table`, or `none`.

space_before_function_open_parenthesis.description: Add a space before `(` in function declarations, e.g. `function foo ()`.

space_before_inline_comment.description: Number of spaces to enforce before inline comments (`--`), or `"keep"` to preserve existing spacing.

space_before_open_square_bracket.description: Add a space before `[` in table index expressions, e.g. `t [key]`.

space_inside_function_call_parentheses.description: Add spaces inside function call parentheses, e.g. `print( 'hello' )`.

space_inside_function_param_list_parenth.description: Add spaces inside function parameter list parentheses, e.g. `function foo( a, b )`.

space_inside_square_brackets.description: Add spaces inside square bracket index expressions, e.g. `t[ 1 ]`.

tab_width.description: Display width of a tab character in spaces, used for visual alignment calculations.

table_separator_style.description: Separator character used between table fields (`none`, `comma`, `semicolon`, `only_kv_colon`).

trailing_table_separator.description: Whether to add or remove a trailing separator after the last field in a table (`keep`, `never`, `always`, `smart`).

annotationsPath.description:
Path to GMod annotations to load as core library.
When set to empty string or not provided, uses VSCode extension's auto-downloaded annotations (if enabled).
Set to explicit path to override, or use `autoLoadAnnotations: false` in .emmyrc to disable entirely.

autoLoadAnnotations.description:
Disable auto-loading of annotations (from VSCode or default path).
This takes precedence over extension settings.

verbosity.description:
Controls how much detail the document outline shows.
- `minimal`: functions, classes, hooks, net.Receive, timers, concommands only.
- `normal` (default): same as minimal plus non-primitive variables and tables; hides
  control-flow blocks.
- `verbose`: everything including `if`, `for`, `do` blocks and all locals.

oneOf.description: Show only functions, classes, VGUI panels, hooks, net receivers, timers, concommands.

Show functions, classes, important tables, hooks, net receivers, timers, concommands, and
non-primitive variables. Hides `if`/`for`/`do` blocks.
Show everything (legacy behavior, includes control-flow blocks and all locals).

customDetail.description:
The detail number of hover information.
Default is `None`, which means using the default detail level.
You can set it to a number between `1` and `255` to customize

closingEndHint.description:
Show an inlay hint after closing `end` keywords indicating what block
they belong to (e.g. function name). Only shown when the block spans
at least `closingEndHintMinLines` lines.

closingEndHintControlFlow.description:
Also show closing `end` hints for control flow blocks (if, while, do,
for). Only effective when `closingEndHint` is enabled.

closingEndHintMinLines.description:
Minimum number of lines a block must span before a closing `end` hint
is shown.

enumParamHint.description:
Show name of enumerator when passing a literal value to a function
that expects an enum.

Example:

```lua
--- @enum Level
local Foo = {
   Info = 1,
   Error = 2,
}

--- @param l Level
function print_level(l) end

print_level(1 --[[ Hint: Level.Info ]])
```

indexHint.description:
Show named array indexes.

Example:

```lua
local array = {
   [1] = 1, -- [name]
}

print(array[1] --[[ Hint: name ]])
```

metaCallHint.description:
Show hint when calling an object results in a call to
its meta table's `__call` function.

overrideHint.description: Show methods that override functions from base class.

paramHint.description: Show parameter names in function calls and parameter types in function definitions.

fuzzySearch.description:
Use fuzzy search when searching for symbol usages
and normal search didn't find anything.

shortStringSearch.description: Also search for usages in strings.

configPrecedence.description: Controls which takes precedence when both `.editorconfig` and `.gluarc.json` formatter settings apply to the same option: `preferEditorconfig` (default) or `preferGluarc`.

externalTool.description: External formatter tool configuration. When set, the specified program is used for formatting instead of the built-in formatter.

externalToolRangeFormat.description: External formatter for range formatting. When set, range format requests use this tool instead of the built-in formatter.

preset.description: Built-in formatter preset. `default` uses EmmyLuaCodeStyle defaults. `cfc` applies the CFC style preset. `custom` applies no preset — only the `styleOverrides` you configure below take effect.

styleOverrides.description: Per-key EmmyLuaCodeStyle style overrides applied on top of the selected preset. Keys not configured here inherit their value from the active preset.

useDiff.description: Whether to use the diff algorithm for formatting.

version.description: Deprecated: GMod-only server always uses LuaJIT. Kept for config compatibility.

renderDocumentationMarkup.description: Render Markdown/RST in documentation. Set `doc.syntax` for this option to have effect.

allowNullableAsNonNullable.description: Allow nullable types (T?) to be passed where non-nullable (T) is expected.

arrayIndex.description: Whether to enable strict mode array indexing.

docBaseConstMatchBaseType.description: Base constant types defined in doc can match base types, allowing int to match `---@alias id 1|2|3`, same for string.

requireExportGlobal.description:
This option limits the visibility of third-party libraries.

When enabled, third-party libraries must use `---@export global` annotation to be importable (i.e., no diagnostic errors and visible in auto-import).

requirePath.description: Whether to enable strict mode require path.

enableReindex.description: Enable full project reindex after changing a file.

library.description:
Library paths. Can be a string path or an object with path and ignore rules.
eg: ["/usr/local/share/lua/5.1"] or [{"path": "/usr/local/share/lua/5.1", "ignoreDir": ["test"], "ignoreGlobs": ["**/*.spec.lua"]}]

packageDirs.description:
Package directories. Treat the parent directory as a `library`, but only add files from the specified directory.
eg: `/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/module`

reindexDuration.description: Delay between changing a file and full project reindex, in milliseconds.
